page: Vultr vbm-6c-128gb is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 2386G processor built on the x86_64 architecture. This CPU provides 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs via hyperthreading running at a base clock speed of 3.5 GHz.

The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of a bundled 1920 GB SSD, providing substantial local capacity for data-intensive operations. No GPU accelerators are included in this configuration. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is provided.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory allocation relative to the 6-core CPU makes this server a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring large memory footprints without needing massive parallel compute scaling. The primary tradeoff is the moderate physical core count, which may limit highly parallelized processing tasks. This hardware profile is suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

Storage is supported by a bundled 1920 GB local SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. With no GPU acceleration, this server offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads. The moderate core count presents a tradeoff for highly parallelized tasks, but the system is well-suited for database hosting, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
